title: Course: COSC 352 - Organization of Programming Languages
description: Explores programming language concepts including syntax, grammars, data type constructs, control structures, data flow, and runtime considerations. Examples drawn from FORTRAN 90, JAVA, ALGOL, SNOBOL 4 and APL.
prerequisites_text: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher
content:
COSC 352 - Organization of Programming Languages
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 220 completed with a grade of C or higher
Offered: Fall, Spring

Description: Explores programming language concepts including syntax, grammars, data type constructs, control structures, data flow, and runtime considerations. Examples drawn from FORTRAN 90, JAVA, ALGOL, SNOBOL 4 and APL.

Curriculum role:
  - BS Computer Science: Core
